Title: Inducible depletion of PI(4,5)P2 by the synthetic iDePP system in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Background evidence cited that PIP5K-mediated PI(4,5)P2 production is involved in development, immunity and reproduction
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PIP5K genes are mentioned as endogenous producers of PI(4,5)P2. 2. Mutations suggest involvement in development, immunity and reproduction. 3. They are contextual genes rather than primary experimental subjects in this study.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned for background context only; no focal experimental dissection of individual PIP5K genes in Results

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Published human OCRL phosphatase systems did not work efficiently in plants; full-length OCRL was avoided due to endogenous targeting interactions
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Human OCRL-based published depletion systems were tested. 2. They were not efficient in plants. 3. Full-length OCRL was not used because it interacts with RABs and clathrin, confounding localization.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background/engineering comparison, not a core experimental gene in the final system

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Published INPP5E phosphatase system did not work efficiently in plants
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. INPP5E-based system was tested during optimization. 2. It was ineffective in plants. 3. It served as a non-working comparator.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Published Inp54-based depletion system did not work efficiently in plants
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Yeast Inp54 was tested as a candidate PI(4,5)P2 phosphatase. 2. It did not function efficiently in plants. 3. It was part of system optimization only.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Published PSEUDOJANIN system did not work efficiently in plants
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PSEUDOJANIN was tested during optimization. 2. It was ineffective in plants. 3. It was not used in the final system.

Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane (synthetically targeted via MAP sequence)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Removes the 5-phosphate from phosphatidylinositol 4,5-bisphosphate to generate PI4P at the plasma membrane
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential for plasma membrane function; required for root growth, root hair elongation, organ initiation, recruitment of endocytic proteins including AP2, clathrin-mediated endocytosis, and cytoskeletal organization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Inducible plasma-membrane-targeted dOCRL rapidly depletes PI(4,5)P2 and causes defects in development, endocytosis, actin dynamics, and microtubule anisotropy
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced after dexamethasone treatment; observed in root epidermis and shoot tissues, with stronger expression in boundary region than central zone of shoot apical meristem under some conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro malachite green phosphatase assay; inducible transgenic expression; biosensor relocalization using mCIT-2xPHPLC; radioactive phosphate labeling/TLC quantification of PIP2; phenotypic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Dexamethasone induction; NPA treatment for naked meristems
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful synthetic tool to acutely manipulate phosphoinositide levels in plants for functional studies rather than direct breeding target
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Engineered chimeric enzyme using only catalytic domain to avoid endogenous targeting/regulation; chosen over plant phosphatases for orthogonality; inactive mutant D468G used as control
